sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects a Windows program executable started from a suspicious folder
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Patrick Bareiss, Anton Kutepov, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects suspicious PowerShell download command
Detects a Powershell process that contains download commands in its command line string
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), oscd.community, Jonhnathan Ribeiro

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Potential Command Line Path Traversal Evasion Attempt
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects potential evasion or obfuscation attempts using bogus path traversal via the commandline

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets - ScriptBlock
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the use of various web request commands with commandline tools and Windows PowerShell cmdlets (including aliases) via PowerShell scriptblock logs
Detects suspicious ways to download files or content using PowerShell

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Execution of Powershell with Base64
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Commandline to launch powershell with a base64 payload

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the use of various web request commands with commandline tools and Windows PowerShell cmdlets (including aliases) via CommandLine
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: James Pemberton / @4A616D6573, Endgame, JHasenbusch, oscd.community, Austin Songer @austinsonger

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious New Instance Of An Office COM Object
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an svchost process spawning an instance of an office application. This happens when the initial word application creates an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc.
This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly. (See vba2clr project in the references)
Detects non-interactive PowerShell activity by looking at the "powershell" process with a non-user GUI process such as "explorer.exe" as a parent.
Detects the creation of a new office macro files on the systems

trid.file_type: Word Microsoft Office Open XML Format document

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects Microsoft Excel spreadsheets that contain hidden sheets. Presence of a hidden sheet alone is not indication of malicious behavior.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Detects suspicious PowerShell code that downloads from web sites
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the execution of a Windows system binary that is usually located in the system folder from an uncommon location.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects potential evasion or obfuscation attempts using bogus path traversal via the commandline
Detects signs of potential use of the WSMAN provider from uncommon processes locally and remote execution.
Detects potential DLL sideloading of "wwlib.dll"
Detects suspicious ways to download files or content using PowerShell
